description: Annual global university rankings published by Times Higher Education, covering the years 2016 to 2026. Each record represents a single university for a single year.

columns.Rank: Numerical rank of the university for the given year. Float because some universities share a rank (e.g. 201.0 for a band).
columns.Name: Full official name of the university.
columns.Country: Country or territory where the university is located.
columns.Student Population: Total number of enrolled students (undergraduate and postgraduate).
columns.Students to Staff Ratio: Ratio of students per academic staff member. Lower values indicate more staff per student.
columns.International Students: Percentage of the student body that are international (non-domestic) students, expressed as a string with a '%' suffix (e.g. '26%').
columns.Female to Male Ratio: Gender split of the student population, expressed as a string in 'F : M' format (e.g. '46 : 54'). Female percentage listed first.
columns.Overall Score: Composite THE score out of 100, aggregating all pillar scores.
columns.Teaching: Teaching pillar score (0-100). Measures the learning environment, including staff-to-student ratio, doctorate-to-bachelor ratio, and institutional reputation for teaching.
columns.Research Environment: Research environment pillar score (0-100). Covers research income, productivity, and reputation.
columns.Research Quality: Research quality pillar score (0-100). Based on citation impact, research strength, and excellence.
columns.Industry Impact: Industry income pillar score (0-100). Measures knowledge transfer — how much industry pays the university for research and consultancy.
columns.International Outlook: International outlook pillar score (0-100). Reflects the proportion of international students, international staff, and international co-authorship.
